name: Beef Kofta in Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 chiles, red or green, fresh, chopped fine
recipeInstructions:
CREAM SAUCE
1 tbsp vegetable oil (I used ghee)
1 tsp cumin seed
2 bay leaves
2 tsp ginger root, chopped fine
2 chiles, red or green, chopped fine
3 garlic cloves
1 1/4 cups whipping cream
1 1/4 tsp coriander seed, ground
1 1/4 tsp cumin seed, ground
1 tsp mango powder
1/2 tsp turmeric, ground
1/2 tsp garam masala
1 bunch cilantro, leaves chopped fine
To make kofta:
1. Place beef, cream, ginger, chilies, garlic, ground coriander, garam masala, cumin, mango powder, and salt to taste in a bowl and mix well to combine.
2. Take spoonfuls of the mixture and, using wet hands, mold the mixture into oval shapes or balls and place them in a steamer.
3. Fill three-quarters full a saucepan with water and bring to a boil. Place the steamer over the saucepan, cover, and steam for 15 to 20 minutes, or until kofta is just cooked.
4. Remove kofta from the steamer and place it in a shallow ovenproof dish. Set aside.
To make sauce:
1. Heat oil in a saucepan over low heat. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, ginger, chilies, and garlic and cook for 2 minutes.
2. Stir in cream, ground coriander, cumin, mango powder, turmeric, and garam masala and cook, stirring constantly, for 5 to 7 minutes.
3. Remove sauce from heat, stir in cilantro, and season with salt to taste.
4. Spoon sauce over kofta, cover, and bake at 300°F for 20 minutes.
The health-conscious can try using spinach in place of the cream. Chop 2 bunches (2 lb) of tender, young spinach. Steam or microwave until tender, drain, place in a food processor or blender, and process until smooth. Use the spinach puree in place of the cream.
